pubmed_links: [Novak et al. (2010) Replicated association of the NR4A3 gene with smoking behaviour in schizophrenia and in bipolar disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20659174/); [He et al. (2024) Potential roles of Nr4a3-mediated inflammation in immunological and neurological diseases](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38261254/); [Corley et al. (2016) Transcriptomic analysis shows decreased cortical expression of NR4A1, NR4A2 and RXRB in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27992436/)
summary: NR4A3 (NOR-1) is an orphan nuclear receptor expressed in dopamine neurons, with SNPs showing replicated association with heavy smoking behavior in patients with schizophrenia and bipolar disorder; NR4A3 modulates hippocampal synaptic plasticity and memory, with potential roles in neuroinflammation. (emerging evidence)
